## Quick orientation for AI coding agents

This repository is a Vite + React (TypeScript) single-page app that uses Supabase as the backend and Supabase Edge Functions (Deno) for server-side integration with Tink (banking). The goal of this document is to surface concrete, discoverable patterns and developer workflows so an AI agent can make safe, correct edits quickly.

Key facts (high level)
- Frontend: `src/` ã React + TypeScript, Tailwind + shadcn-ui components under `src/components` and `src/components/ui`.
- Routing: `src/App.tsx` ã react-router v6; protected routes wrap pages with `ProtectedRoute`.
- State / server data: TanStack React Query (`@tanstack/react-query`) is used centrally via `QueryClientProvider` in `src/App.tsx`.
- Auth: Supabase auth is used. Client helper: `src/integrations/supabase/client.ts` (auto-generated) and `src/hooks/useAuth.ts` implements the app's auth lifecycle and role fetch from `user_roles`.
- Server: Supabase Edge Functions are in `supabase/functions/*` (Deno TypeScript). They use `createClient` and expect `SUPABASE_URL` / `SUPABASE_ANON_KEY` from environment.
- Database/migrations: `supabase/migrations/*.sql` ã contains table schemas (notably `tink_users`, `tink_accounts`, `bank_transactions`, `user_roles`) and RLS policies.

Important patterns and rules to follow (concrete)
- Do not edit generated files. `src/integrations/supabase/client.ts` has a header: "This file is automatically generated. Do not edit it directly." If you need type changes, find the generator that produced it.
- Auth ordering: `src/hooks/useAuth.ts` registers `onAuthStateChange` before calling `supabase.auth.getSession()` ã keep this ordering when adjusting auth logic to avoid race conditions. The hook intentionally defers role fetch with `setTimeout` to avoid deadlocks.
- Routes: Add new pages under `src/pages` and register them in `src/App.tsx` above the catch-all `*` route (see the inline comment in `App.tsx`).
- UI primitives: Use components from `src/components/ui/*` (shadcn-based) and `cn(...)` from `src/lib/utils.ts` to compose Tailwind classnames consistently.
- Data imports/upserts: Server-side functions and sync code (e.g., `supabase/functions/tink-sync-all/index.ts`) use `upsert` for idempotent writes; follow the same approach when inserting bank/tink-related rows (use unique keys like `tink_account_id` / `tink_transaction_id`).

Integration points to be careful with
- Tink integration: Edge functions in `supabase/functions/*` call the Tink API using access tokens stored in `tink_users`. Changes to these functions must consider RLS policies and the fact they run under the Supabase runtime (Deno). Example: `supabase/functions/tink-sync-all/index.ts`.
- Supabase RLS: Migrations enable Row Level Security. Prefer using Supabase clients (with the user's Authorization header) to let RLS enforce access, rather than bypassing via a service role.
- Secrets and environment: The frontend uses a publishable key and the Edge Functions rely on `Deno.env`. Do not commit service-role secrets. When testing locally, use the Supabase CLI and env vars (`SUPABASE_URL`, `SUPABASE_ANON_KEY`, etc.).

Developer workflows (concrete commands)
- Install & run dev server (frontend):
  - npm i
  - npm run dev  (Vite server runs on port 8080, host configured as `::` in `vite.config.ts`)
- Build / preview:
  - npm run build
  - npm run preview
- Linting: `npm run lint` (ESLint is configured in repo root)
- Supabase local/testing (inferred from `supabase/` folder): use the Supabase CLI to run migrations / functions locally. Typical steps (requires supabase CLI):
  - supabase start
  - supabase functions serve <function-name>
  - supabase db reset / supabase db push  (migrations live in `supabase/migrations`)
  (Note: the repo contains `supabase/config.toml` which indicates local supabase usage.)

Conventions observed (how code is organized)
- Features grouped by folder type: UI components `src/components`, page screens `src/pages`, hooks in `src/hooks`, small helpers in `src/lib` and `utils`.
- API surface split: frontend talks to Supabase client (`src/integrations/supabase/client.ts`) and also calls Edge Functions in `supabase/functions/*` for third-party integrations (Tink), rather than calling Tink from the browser.
- Centralized query client: use the global `QueryClient` (App-level) for server state; individual data hooks use React Query patterns (see `src/hooks/useBankTransactions.ts` etc.). When adding a new data hook, follow the established pattern of encapsulating fetching/mutations in `src/hooks`.

What AI agents should do first when changing code
1. Check whether the target file is auto-generated (`src/integrations/supabase/client.ts`) or part of `supabase/functions/` (server-side Deno). Avoid editing generated files; update the generator or types instead.
2. For auth or sync changes, inspect `src/hooks/useAuth.ts`, `src/components/ProtectedRoute.tsx`, and related migrations in `supabase/migrations` to ensure client changes match DB schema and RLS.
3. When adding network calls to third-party services, prefer adding them to `supabase/functions/*` (server-side) and call those functions from the frontend. This pattern is used for Tink integration.

Reference files (good starting points)
- `src/App.tsx` ã central routing & providers
- `src/hooks/useAuth.ts` ã auth lifecycle and role fetching
- `src/integrations/supabase/client.ts` ã generated Supabase client (do not edit)
- `supabase/functions/tink-sync-all/index.ts` ã example edge function integrating Tink -> Supabase
- `supabase/migrations/*.sql` ã schema, RLS and indexes

If anything in this short guide is unclear or you want more detail (local supabase workflow, where types are generated, or the CI/deploy steps), tell me which area to expand and I'll iterate.
